sentence_reviews.text: Batching and admission control combine requests, assign accelerator time, enforce priorities, and balance latency, throughput, fairness, and memory pressure.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: DDIA supports batching, scheduling, backpressure/load, and distributed service tradeoffs; accelerator memory pressure and GPU scheduling are synthesized beyond the cited source.
sentence_reviews.notes: Core scheduling/batching concept is supported; GPU-specific details need an inference serving or GPU scheduler source.

sentence_reviews.text: Operational gains introduce new failure modes in concurrency, versioning, security, numerical behavior, observability, and cost. Its direct dependencies include Model servers & inference runtimes, Distributed systems & networking.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Batching, admission control & GPU scheduling is modeled as depending on Model servers & inference runtimes, Distributed systems & networking and feeding downstream nodes such as Paged attention & KV-memory management, Fault tolerance, security, energy & cost optimization.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.
